description: Interview questions for AI application engineers covering LLM apps, RAG systems, agents, APIs, observability, deployment, and reliability.

questions.content: How would you design an AI mock interview system that generates questions from a job description and a resume?
reference_points: Parse JD into a skill matrix with skill weights, categories, and required flags
Parse resume into projects, skills, highlights, links, and risk signals
Use RAG to retrieve relevant interview references instead of stuffing the whole question bank
Use structured output schemas for question plans, assessments, and reports
Maintain interview state with graph nodes for analysis, planning, interviewing, and evaluation
follow_up_directions: How would you handle weak resume parsing quality?
How would you evaluate whether generated questions match the JD?

questions.content: What are the main risks when using LLMs to generate interview questions automatically?
reference_points: Questions may drift away from the JD or candidate background
The model may invent resume details or overfit to a single project
Difficulty may be inconsistent across candidates
Reference answers can be incomplete or misleading if sources are poor
Mitigations include structured prompts, RAG references, source tracking, and offline evaluation
follow_up_directions: How would you detect hallucinated resume details?
How would you control question difficulty?

questions.content: How would you design streaming responses for an AI interview chat service?
reference_points: Use WebSocket or SSE depending on bidirectional interaction requirements
Stream partial LLM tokens to reduce perceived latency
Persist conversation state and current question index outside the connection object
Handle disconnects, retries, and idempotent message processing
Separate transport protocol messages from internal agent state
follow_up_directions: When would you choose SSE instead of WebSocket?
How would you resume an interrupted interview session?

questions.content: How would you implement structured output parsing for LLM-generated interview plans?
reference_points: Define strict Pydantic models for question items and plans
Constrain fields such as difficulty, skill tags, source ids, and reference points
Validate model output before saving it into graph state
Retry or repair invalid outputs using schema errors
Keep prompts explicit about array sizes, allowed values, and source constraints
follow_up_directions: How would you handle partial model output?
What fields should be required in a question plan?

questions.content: How would you evaluate the quality of an AI-generated interview question plan?
reference_points: Measure JD skill coverage and required skill coverage
Check question diversity across categories and difficulty levels
Verify source ids when questions are adapted from RAG references
Use human review rubrics for relevance, depth, and fairness
Track candidate answer assessment consistency across similar questions
follow_up_directions: How would you build an automatic eval dataset?
How would you prevent the plan from asking too many questions about one skill?

questions.content: What is the difference between a workflow-based LLM app and an agentic LLM app?
reference_points: A workflow has a mostly predefined path and deterministic control flow
An agent can choose tools or next actions based on intermediate observations
Graph frameworks can combine deterministic nodes with agentic decision points
Workflow systems are easier to test and debug
Agentic systems are useful when the next step depends on uncertain external information
follow_up_directions: Which parts of an interview system should be deterministic?
Where would you allow the model to make decisions?

questions.content: How would you use LangGraph to model a multi-stage AI interview pipeline?
reference_points: Represent JD analysis, resume analysis, question planning, interviewing, evaluation, and reporting as nodes
Use typed shared state to pass skill matrix, resume profile, question plan, and assessments
Use conditional edges to switch between practice and professional modes
Use reducers for append-only fields such as messages and assessments
Keep side effects isolated in nodes for easier testing
follow_up_directions: How would you debug a graph state issue?
How would you support two interview rounds?

questions.content: How would you design session management for multiple concurrent AI interviews?
reference_points: Use a session id to isolate interview state per candidate
Store durable state in a database or checkpoint store instead of process memory only
Use Redis for short-lived session metadata and rate limiting
Avoid sharing mutable global objects across sessions
Define cleanup rules for abandoned sessions and uploaded resumes
follow_up_directions: What breaks if the service has multiple workers?
How would you implement session expiration?

questions.content: How would you add observability to an AI interview backend?
reference_points: Log request ids, session ids, node names, model names, and latency
Track token usage and cost per interview session
Capture retrieval queries, source ids, and top-k scores for RAG debugging
Record structured errors for parsing, retrieval, LLM calls, and WebSocket events
Use dashboards for latency, error rate, and model output validation failures
follow_up_directions: What logs should not contain user data?
How would you debug a bad generated question?

questions.content: How would you design rate limiting and abuse protection for an LLM-powered interview API?
reference_points: Apply per-user, per-IP, and per-session rate limits
Limit uploaded file size and accepted file types
Set maximum interview length, token budget, and retry count
Use queueing or backpressure when model providers are slow
Monitor abnormal usage patterns and failed validation rates
follow_up_directions: How would you protect against prompt injection in resumes?
How would you handle provider rate limits?

questions.content: How would you prevent prompt injection from a candidate's resume or job description?
reference_points: Treat JD and resume text as untrusted data, not instructions
Use clear prompt boundaries and label user-provided content
Extract structured facts before using them in downstream prompts
Validate generated outputs against schema and source constraints
Avoid allowing uploaded text to override system policies or tool usage rules
follow_up_directions: Can schema validation fully solve prompt injection?
How would you test prompt injection cases?

questions.content: How would you handle resume PDF parsing quality in an AI interview product?
reference_points: Use deterministic text extraction first, then LLM normalization
Detect empty pages, garbled text, tables, and missing project sections
Return parse warnings and confidence signals to downstream agents
Keep original text snippets for traceability
Allow fallback manual input when parsing quality is poor
follow_up_directions: How would you parse links and GitHub URLs?
How would you evaluate resume parser quality?

questions.content: How would you match a candidate resume to a job description before question generation?
reference_points: Normalize skill names and aliases from both JD and resume
Map projects to skills based on tech stack and descriptions
Identify matched, missing, and unclear required skills
Use matching results to select project deep-dive questions
Use missing skills to create fair but focused probing questions
follow_up_directions: How would you handle skill aliases like JS and JavaScript?
How would you avoid penalizing a candidate for wording differences?

questions.content: How would you design a two-round professional interview flow with LLM agents?
reference_points: Round 1 focuses on resume project depth and JD core skills
After Round 1, summarize strengths, weaknesses, and areas to probe
Round 2 uses the summary to broaden technical coverage
Store separate question plans and assessments for each round
Generate a final report that combines both rounds
follow_up_directions: How would you stop Round 2 from repeating Round 1?
What fields should a Round 1 summary contain?

questions.content: How would you make AI-generated interview assessments more consistent?
reference_points: Use a fixed scoring rubric with explicit score ranges
Provide the question, reference points, and candidate answer together
Require structured assessment fields such as score, strengths, gaps, and follow-up need
Calibrate with golden candidate answers
Monitor score distribution and disagreement across model versions
follow_up_directions: How would you compare two evaluator prompts?
How would you detect scoring inflation?

questions.content: How would you design fallback behavior when the LLM provider fails during an interview?
reference_points: Use timeouts and bounded retries for provider calls
Return graceful error messages without losing session state
Fallback to cached question plans or simpler generation modes when possible
Persist partial progress before long-running calls
Track provider error rates and switch providers only with compatible schemas
follow_up_directions: What should happen if assessment generation fails?
How would you make retries idempotent?

questions.content: How would you store and retrieve interview conversation history efficiently?
reference_points: Store structured messages with role, content, timestamp, and question id
Keep recent turns in active state and archive full history in durable storage
Summarize long history for downstream reporting when needed
Separate candidate answers from system and interviewer messages
Avoid sending the entire history to every LLM call if it is not needed
follow_up_directions: When would you summarize conversation history?
How would you support exporting a transcript?

questions.content: How would you design a model abstraction layer for an AI application?
reference_points: Encapsulate provider-specific API clients behind a common interface
Support chat completion, structured output, and embedding separately
Keep model names, base URLs, and API keys in configuration
Normalize provider errors into application-level exceptions
Make tests use fake clients instead of real network calls
follow_up_directions: How would you switch from one provider to another?
How would you test structured output parsing?

questions.content: How would you handle cost control for an LLM-based interview product?
reference_points: Track token usage per session and per graph node
Limit maximum rounds, follow-ups, and report length
Use smaller models for extraction or classification when quality is sufficient
Cache deterministic or repeated outputs such as JD analysis when appropriate
Use RAG to keep prompts focused rather than stuffing full corpora
follow_up_directions: Where would caching be risky?
How would you estimate cost before starting an interview?

questions.content: How would you design versioning for prompts and evaluation rubrics?
reference_points: Store prompt templates with explicit versions
Log prompt version, model version, and schema version for each run
Use golden cases to compare prompt changes
Separate system instructions from dynamic user context
Roll out changes gradually and monitor quality metrics
follow_up_directions: How would you rollback a bad prompt update?
What metrics would you compare between prompt versions?

questions.content: How would you design file upload handling for resumes in a FastAPI service?
reference_points: Validate file type, size, and extension before parsing
Store uploads in a controlled directory with generated names
Avoid trusting original filenames
Clean up temporary files after parsing or session expiration
Return structured parse errors to the client
follow_up_directions: How would you prevent path traversal?
How would you support DOCX and PDF?

questions.content: How would you design a frontend chat UI for an AI interview session?
reference_points: Show current question, progress, skill tags, and difficulty
Stream interviewer messages and preserve candidate answers
Handle loading, reconnecting, and error states clearly
Disable duplicate submissions while an answer is being processed
Provide report and transcript views after completion
follow_up_directions: How would you handle reconnect in the UI?
What state should be kept on the client versus server?

questions.content: How would you test a LangGraph-based AI interview workflow?
reference_points: Unit test deterministic helpers such as skill matching and query building
Use fake LLM clients for graph node tests
Validate state transitions for practice and professional modes
Test schema validation failures and retry paths
Run end-to-end smoke tests with a small sample JD and resume
follow_up_directions: How would you avoid flaky tests with real LLM calls?
Which parts should be integration tested?

questions.content: How would you design graceful degradation when RAG retrieval returns poor results?
reference_points: Detect low scores, empty results, or low source diversity
Fallback to skill-matrix-only generation when retrieval is unavailable
Use broader queries or category-level retrieval as a second attempt
Tell the planner when references are weak instead of pretending they are reliable
Log poor retrieval cases for offline dataset improvement
follow_up_directions: What threshold would you use for low confidence?
How would you improve the question bank based on failures?

questions.content: How would you design source tracking for generated interview questions?
reference_points: Expose source ids and source categories in the structured question model
Only allow source ids that were actually retrieved in the current planning call
Track child chunk ids when using parent-child retrieval
Use source tracking for debugging and offline quality review
Leave source fields empty for fully original questions
follow_up_directions: How would you prevent the model from inventing source ids?
How would source tracking help with evaluation?
